interactions.summary: Jiang continues the Jessica Lynch comparison: U.S. forces stage rescue spectacle because looking good matters more than winning. Sneako calls Iran a sunken-cost fallacy and asks about the draft; Jiang predicts economic disruption, rationing, National Guard control, and draft pressure.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Sneako asks or frames the issue; Jiang answers in the main analytic voice where present.

speaker_notes.note: Machine diarization is consistent in this packet: SPEAKER_00 is Sneako/interviewer and SPEAKER_01 is Jiang, except where intro/outro material is host-only.

claims.claim: Jiang uses the Jessica Lynch story to argue that American military spectacle can fabricate danger to produce heroic rescue narratives.
He says war has its own logical momentum: once inside a war, every new problem becomes a reason to escalate rather than a reason to leave.
He warns that within six months the Iran war could bring air-defense collapse, rationing, national draft pressure, and National Guard deployments inside America.

signature_moments.moment: War’s logic is circular: create the problem, then invade to solve the problem created by bombing.
signature_moments.source_phrase: war has a logical momentum of its own
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It compresses the escalation model that drives the episode.
